title: ## **Balance Sheet Overview – As of March 31, 2026**
content:
DATA CHECK: The source table is titled 'BOARD REPORT' with a footer date of December 31, 2026. However, the data columns are labeled for Q1 2025 through Q1 2026 (March 31, 2026). Per Gate 4 instructions to use figures from the source table and permate the title format given above with the actual date filled in: The section will be titled 'Balance Sheet Overview – As of March 31, 2026' using the Q1 2026 data points.

The most significant balance-sheet development this period was a substantial increase in cash and deposits. Total assets stood at $10,493 million as of March 31, 2026, increased 1.6%.

Cash and Deposits grew to $81,7 million, up 12.9%, creating a larger near-term liquidity position. Investment Securities rose to $2,204 million, up 2.7%. Loans and Leases moved more modestly, ending the period at $6,628 million.

Loans and leases is now the largest component of the balance sheet. That growth on the asset side was funded primarily through shares/deposits, reflecting how the institution's holdings were paid for during the period.

Total liabilities stood at $8,505 million, increased 1.3%, led by accounts payable, accrued interest on borrowings, and other liabilities at $3,7 million, up 40.9%. Shares/deposits moved more modestly over the period.

Total equity stood at $878 million, increased 1.8%, supported by undivided earnings of $934 million and growing reserves. Taken together, the increase in assets was funded mainly through member deposits while retained capital grew. Management continues to watch the pace of loan growth relative to deposit growth.
